comment_content:
Basic Java
1. Using BlueJ
2. Strings and Loops
3. Class and arrays
4. File I/O and Recursion
5. Greenfoot

the concept of class and arrays are quite useful for 2XXX COMP courses like COMP2011/2012. Somehow better than 1029C for preparation for COMP2011. (Why they don't use Java to teach OOP tho...)

If you cannot enroll due to not finishing COMP1021 by the time applying, just waive pre requisite and state you are taking COMP1021, Gibson is nice and he will usually allow you to enroll.
comment_teaching:
The canvas page might seems to be redundant for pros, but quite clear and easy to read for a Java Rookie like me.

No lessons, just read canvas page online
comment_workload:
simple HWs, can be done within 1-2 hours each week even in month-long Winter/Summer semester

Just ChatGPT or Deepseek the assignment if you have really no idea (just kidding don't, email TAs or find you peers for help instead)
comment_grading:
Pass / Fail course
40% in exam
60% in HWs (4 in total) to pass
cheat sheets can be used in final

Final is not too hard and you should be able to pass if you do some basic preparation

comment_content:
4 (actually 5, but the first is an intro) chapters in total: basic java stuffs using mainly BlueJ, and also last chapter Greenfoot;

However organization of notes is not good, some lengthy examples are tiring to read, kind of unnecessary too,
Just fking tell me the syntax and it's good enough;

Any online java lesson would probably be better than this course;

Also you cannot learn or do new homework until it unlocks the chapter, why, Gibson ?
comment_teaching:
no any lesson, just self-study (reading from notes, no videos), maybe learn from other online resources to get better understanding.

Also is Gibson so lazy? Why give homework grades so late, but give final grades so fast (just 1 day after exam) ?? Maybe he don't want to work in Chinese New year I guess.
comment_workload: Nearly no workload at all, can easily complete whole chapter in just 1 day, each homework (exercise) have about a week for you to work with, but actually only need like 15 minutes as it's just fill in the blanks if you  fast enough. 4 Homework in total.
comment_grading:
Very easy to pass:
get 60+/100 for 4 homework (each 25%)
0 effort needed to get full marks

and 40+/100 in 1 hour final exam
also very easy, even without cheat sheet
the average is like 74 ?

comment_content:
The course includes some basic knowledge of Java, e.g. loops, methods (called functions in other programming language), BlueJ, etc.
as long as you have had a touch in programming, the contents should be easy to you (and it's okay even if you had never)
You would make a little game at the end of the course and it is fun to see the result coming out.
After the course, you would know why Java is called object-oriented.
comment_teaching:
The contents are taught by texts and some examples. There is no 'teacher', so there's nothing to be graded tbh.
but the teaching materials are written clearly so that you could understand just by reading it.
i believe you could still seek out to ask questions if you have any, but i did not contact the course instructor throughout the course so i'm not sure about it.
comment_workload:
again, your goal is to get a passing score
finishing four tasks is def not difficult or time-consuming
each task took me less than an hour to finish (the first two even took less)
final exam is open-note, you could bring your own notes in hardcopy (and i rmb there was no page limit? i brought ard 3 A4 pages, if there is a limit, it should be something close to this ), no electronic devices allowed
comment_grading:
This course is pass/ fail only, so your goal is to pass (60% in total), not to answer every question or finish every task perfectly
The course includes 4 graded tasks and one final exam.
What you need to do is finish at least two (or three, sorry i dont remember so well) out of four tasks, and get 60% or above including final exam

comment_content: Basics about Java is included in this course, and the tools used in this course is BlueJ and Greenfoot.
comment_teaching: This is an online self paced course, so just read the notes and materials in the canvas. The materials are clear and easy to understand as well.
comment_workload: The 4 assignments have the skeleton code and the instruction for the assignment is very clear as well. Hence, the workload is really low compare with other coding course (as it is an 1 credit course of course)
comment_grading:
4 Assignment + Final Exam
The assignment and exam are easy as it is just a bridging course. As this is an online course, this is an open book and open notes exam.

comment_content:
Learning about basic Java programming which features the use of  BlueJ and Greenfoot. Suitable for those who are interested in learning Java and are looking for a starting point to dive into the Java world. Not suitable for those who have already learned the basics and looking for advanced content.

Rough syllabus:
1. Data types, operators, use of Java package
2. Print text and get input from users
3. If, Switch
4. For loop, While loop, Do While loop
5. Object-oriented programming in Java(class, method, instance)
6. Array in Java
7. Inheritance & Interface
8. Types of error, catching exceptions
9. Using path, read and write a file
10. Recursion
11. Greenfoot section(making a game)
comment_teaching:
Self-learning, self-pacing. You need to read through the materials on Canvas on your own. The amount of material is not heavy, about 3-4 sections every week with several examples. If you are hardworking enough, learning through all of these would be a piece of cake. Recommend actually downloading BlueJ and Greenfoot and following the examples to have a better understanding.
If you could not push yourself to study on your own, this course may not suit you.
comment_workload:
Very little workload. Only 3-4 pieces of material to learn every week, along with 1 piece of homework.

About the homework :
1. Gave you a template. You only need to fill in specific parts, just like in COMP1021.
2. Only 3-4 simple tasks. You may check your program with the given example.
3. Most of the part is copied and pasted from the example. If not, from the previous learning material. You may always find TA  for help if you really could not figure it out.
4. Make sure you check the requirement carefully.
5. Make sure you submit the correct file. (.java or .zip, depending on the homework)
comment_grading:
A Pass or Fail course. If you achieve:
1. Submitting at least 3 homework and having good scores among them
2. Getting at least 40% on the final exam
Then you would pass the course.

About the final exam:
1. Open book, open notes. So do not hesitate to make some cheat sheets to bring to your exam.
2. Mixed with MCs and Short Questions. Does not require you to write a whole program on your own suddenly.
3. Most questions are straightforward and basic. Don't be stressed - Getting 40% is all you need!

comment_content:
The contents are good but not very deep, which include some basics of Java, basic OOP, Error handling, File I/O, and probably the least useful: Greenfoot.

The course suggests you to use BlueJ for a better visualization of classes and methods but I prefer to use vscode. Still you need to know how to use BlueJ as there are exam questions on BlueJ.
comment_teaching: There's no teaching at all. You just go through the canvas pages and read the materials yourself.
comment_workload: It's a 1-credit course so there's really not much workload. Every assignments can be finished within 2 hours with the guidelines from the assignment.
comment_grading:
Easy to pass the course. Just get >60% on assignment and >40%  on final exam (which should be open book) .

I got 3/4 assignment full mark and 1 assignment 0 mark because I submitted the wrong file, and I got 72% in final exam.

comment_content:
Content is just a very simple introduction to java, designed for someone with no experience. Uses BlueJ and GreenFoot, not practical but good for beginners to visualise what they're making.

The content is presented on Canvas, and is very easy to get through (only takes like an hour maximum). There are five of these pages overall, which results in only a few hours of content for a full semester. However, this is probably in line with most other 1-credit courses.
comment_teaching: There is no teacher for the course, so cannot say. I hear Gibson is a good one though.
comment_workload:
Basically non-existent. The final exam takes like an hour of revision to pass, and was quite easy. The assignments are all quite straightforward, and just use what you've learnt from the canvas pages.
There is around a few hours of work in this course that you need to do, so very good, can finish the entire course's content in a day.
comment_grading: It is a pass/fail course so the grading is no problem. Passing involved the exam and assignments which are both very easy to do well in.

comment_content: It is a course which will bring you through the basics of java, like hello world, basic data handling and stuff. 5 Lessons in total, you will have spare time to finish each of them with half-a-day. Starting from lesson 4, you will learn some basic of OOP, which includes Inheritance, Interfaces, error handling, file input and output or even coordinate handling in a 2D world. But again, easy stuff, what do you expect from a 1-credit course and the best grade you can get is a Pass.
comment_teaching: Learn by yourself, only words are there to read and thus to teach. If you are really really struggling, you could contact TA -- Google. There're actually some real TAs and I'd say Google could do the job much faster.
comment_workload: I didn't expect to create 5 programs in a 1-credit course, so I rate it a C.
comment_grading: Easy to pass, impossible to get A. You only need to identify the syntax errors in the codes in the final. And in some extreme cases, a dry run table will get you through all the problems.

comment_content:
The content is easy and more concentrates on the usage of blueJ.
BuleJ is helpful
comment_teaching: There is no lecture and we are required to do self studying. You can ask the TA for help if you have questions.
comment_workload: There is little workload.What you need to do is illustrated clearly.
comment_grading: It is a p/f course. Programming exercises: submit and obtain 60% or higher of the four exercises total mark. That means you could submit three out of four exercises and still pass the requirement, given that you get very good marks for them. Final examination: obtain 40% or higher in the final exam

comment_content:
Am I the only one who thought this can be counted in the best 24 cred in the major selection and enrolled in this course? 🤔 (It is P/F so it don't count :P)

Simple Java using BlueJ and Greenfoot.
learn it yourself by reading the notes on Canvas. It is a Canvas page, not pdf files. There are 5 parts. Part 2~5 have a homework in the end so 4 homework in total. He unlocks 1 part every month and the homework is due before he unlocks the next part so you can't finish learning everything at once.
comment_teaching: The homework and example are easy to follow through and clear to read. but I wonder if I can remember anything after the final exam. :P So, I didn't rate it an A for teaching.
comment_workload: One part of notes and one homework per month. 3 hours per month will be enough. per month not per week so the workload is very light.
comment_grading:
It is just a Pass/Fail Course and I don't think there is anyone who failed this course

He literally wrote “There are enough easy problems to let you reach the passing mark (40%)” in the final exam. (it is an open book exam so think about it)

comment_teaching:
No face to face lesson.

All the teaching materials are on Canvas. Read all of them by yourselves.

I have asked the professor for a few times. His explanations are pretty well. Usually, he will reply you in less than 24 hours.

Just make sure you do enough hand-in exercise and do normally in exam to get enough score to pass.

There are also some review questions that you may do. However, they will not counted towards your final grade and it is optional for you to do them.
comment_workload:
Very light. I took this course when I was Year 1.

Just make sure you hand in your work before the deadline, and do some revision before the final exam, you will get a pass.

If you do not have anything to do in winter semester, I highly recommend you to enroll this course so that you can learn something meaningful.
comment_grading:
This is a Pass/Fail course. To pass the course, you need to:

1.
Programming exercises:
submit and obtain 60% or higher of the four exercises total mark. That means you could submit three out of four exercises and still pass the requirement, given that you get very good marks for them.

2.
Final examination:
obtain 40% or higher in the final exam.


The final exam contains 20 questions. It lasts for 1 hour. Some of the questions are multiple-choice questions and some of them are short questions. The exam is open book and open notes (digital or paper-based).


My result:

Exercise for Lesson 2 (ASCII to Emoji) :
100/100

Exercise for Lesson 3 (Dim Sum Ordering System) :
100/100

Exercise for Lesson 4 (A Phone Book Program) :
100/100

Exercise for Lesson 5 (A Shooting Game) :
100/100

Final Exam :
87/100

Grade: P

(For your reference, I took COMP1021 in 2021-2022 Fall and I got A+ at last.)

I also got Level 5* in 2021 HKDSE ICT. My elective is Software Development. The programming language I learned in secondary school was C.

In the same semester (2021-2022 Winter), I also took COMP1029C because the syntax of both languages are quite similar, and I pass all these courses eventually.)

comment_content:
Using BlueJ / Greenfoot to write some simple program.
The content are just similar to other programming language, nothing really special.
comment_teaching:
online self-study without lessons, so no comment.
You will be provided notes for the course (maybe with video sometimes).
comment_workload:
-Online notes
-Assignments()
-Final exam

You may need to spend some time reading the notes before you do the exercise or assignments.

Assignment: 2.5~3.5 week per assignment

Final exam: close-book exam. Just for getting a passing mark to pass the course, so it's not hard at all

BUT!!!
This course may still be a burden during Spring/Fall sem. Taking this course during winter/summer maybe a better option.
comment_grading:
Only P/F for this course. If you can finish all the assignment and doing the final exam, you would probably get a pass for this course.
Required a passing mark in both assignment and final exam.

comment_content: An intro course to Java programming, starting from basic syntax to preliminary OOP. The course is on BlueJ and Greenfoot, both are easy to handle. Just follow step by step and nothing will be difficult.
comment_teaching: Self-learning... Nice slides by the way.
comment_workload: Well, you can complete the slides and quizzes of the week altogether in 2 hours or less. Half a day of review before the exam will be enough to get it passed.  No extra effort is required.
comment_grading:
pass and fail only.
>60 % in the lab
>40% in the final

comment_content: Quite simple with prior programming experience but nice to get a feel of the JAVA language.
comment_teaching:
No personal teaching, just read the course website. Projects are provided for each topic to exemplify the point.
TA marks the assignment but with minimal feedback.
comment_workload: Very light workload, on average half an hour per week. Only five lessons in the entire semester. Since this is a P/F subject, not much revision required for finals also.
comment_grading:
Complete a minimum number out of all assignments and achieve at least 40% in finals.

Assignments are simple as step-by-step instructions are given. The final assignment is a bit longer but still very detailed instructions with a video from Gibson.

The final is quite simple especially if you have experience with COMP 1021 style exams. Less about JAVA syntax, more on programming concepts.
